title: Pungent Kenyan Beef Broth
description: A hearty and flavorful Kenyan-inspired broth featuring beef stock and a crispy, spicy kick. Great for a sick day or whenever you need a comforting bowl of goodness.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, ginger, and lemongrass and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the beef stock, kombu seaweed, black peppercorns, crushed red pepper flakes, and ketchup. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the apple cider vinegar and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Season the broth with salt to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro just before serving.
Serve with a side of crusty bread or over rice for a heartier meal.
You can adjust the level of spiciness to your liking by adding more or less crushed red pepper flakes.
You can also add some diced chicken or beef to make it a more filling meal.
